text.title: docker-compose Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for docker-compose. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Omit the top-level `version:` field. Modern Compose V2 ignores it; it causes version-mismatch rejections with older tooling.
rule.rationale: The Compose spec no longer requires a version field. Including it creates confusion and can block valid files in some older CI tools.

rule.statement: Set `container_name` explicitly on every service so log queries and exec commands use a stable name.
rule.rationale: Without a fixed name, Compose generates names from project + service + index, which changes across recreations.

rule.statement: Set `restart: unless-stopped` (staging) or `restart: always` (production) on every long-running service.
rule.rationale: Without a restart policy, a crashed container stays down until manually restarted.

rule.statement: Define a `healthcheck` on every service that is referenced by another service's `depends_on` with `condition: service_healthy`. If healthcheck is absent, the condition is silently ignored and the dependent starts immediately.
rule.rationale: Race conditions on startup (app tries to connect to DB before it is ready) are the leading cause of intermittent Compose failures.

rule.statement: Bind exposed ports to `127.0.0.1:HOST_PORT:CONTAINER_PORT` for services that must not be publicly reachable. Docker rewrites iptables directly and bypasses UFW.
rule.rationale: A port bound to `0.0.0.0` is reachable from the internet regardless of UFW rules, because Docker inserts ACCEPT rules before UFW's DROP rules.

rule.statement: Mark backend networks as `internal: true` so containers on that network cannot reach the internet directly.
rule.rationale: Limits blast radius of a compromised container — it cannot exfiltrate data or download payloads.

rule.statement: Use named volumes for all persistent data in production. Bind mounts are only for config files (`:ro`) or development source sync.
rule.rationale: Named volumes are managed by Docker, survive container recreation, and have better performance on Linux than bind mounts on macOS/Windows.

rule.statement: Set `deploy.resources.limits.memory` and `.cpus` on every service. Without limits a runaway container can OOM-kill the host.
rule.rationale: Docker on Linux applies cgroup limits only when they are declared; no limit means unbounded resource use.

rule.statement: Configure log rotation on every service: `driver: json-file` with `max-size: "10m"` and `max-file: "3"`. Without rotation, container logs fill the disk indefinitely.
rule.rationale: The default json-file driver writes unbounded logs to `/var/lib/docker/containers/`.

rule.statement: Store all secrets in `.env` (gitignored). Commit only `.env.example` with placeholder values. Never put plaintext secrets in the compose file's `environment:` block.
rule.rationale: `docker compose config` and `docker inspect` expose `environment:` values in plain text.

rule.statement: Use profiles (`profiles: [dev]`) for optional services (adminer, mailhog, monitoring) so `docker compose up -d` starts only the production set.
rule.rationale: Eliminates the need for separate compose files for dev vs prod optional services.
